stories.headline: Seattle's Backfield Runs Wide Open: A Lead Job Nobody's Staked Yet
stories.read: Well pardner, with Kenneth Walker long gone to Kansas City and Zach Charbonnet still nursin' a busted wing (that ACL from January), Seattle's got a lead-back job sittin' out on the open range with no clear owner. Coach smiled and said Charbonnet by Week 1 is 'everything's possible,' but don't bet the ranch on it, which leaves rookie Jadarian Price and George Holani circlin' the same watering hole (Holani took the first-team reins at minicamp when the whole saloon figured it'd be Price). That's a herd of unclaimed totes waitin' on whoever strikes first. Grab a late-round dart on both before your outfit catches wind, 'cause one of 'em's fixin' to gush.

stories.headline: Ladd McConkey's Vein Reopens: McDaniel Rides In, Keenan Allen Rides Out
stories.read: Hold up, this one's a strike waitin' to happen. The Chargers handed McConkey's wagon to new play-caller Mike McDaniel (the fella who feeds shifty little burners a steady diet of paydirt), and they let Keenan Allen walk, cuttin' 122 throws loose for the takin'. McConkey slumped some in '25 (789 yards after a 1,149-yard rookie strike), so the saloon's got him priced cheaper than he oughta be, around the fourth turn of the great land grab. That's a low-end No. 1 ceiling at a No. 2 price, pardner. Cheap gold, go pan it.

stories.headline: Mike Evans Rides Into San Fran, and Pearsall's the Cheap Gold Behind Him
stories.read: The 49ers hauled in future Hall-of-Famer Mike Evans to line up beside Ricky Pearsall, and Brock Purdy's the kind of sharpshooter who turns that into points (the man's finished top-seven in efficiency every year he's slung it). Evans is still the closer near paydirt, a fine No. 3 you can wait on 'til the middle rounds, but the real diamond in the rough is Pearsall, penciled in as the starter opposite him with breakout whispers ridin' off the wire. Stake a late claim on that young fella, and if Evans' old legs take a spill, he inherits a mountain of throws.
